attributes.description: Alerts when used storage exceeds 60% of the total capacity on a store (`capacity_used / capacity > 0.6`). CockroachDB recommends keeping used below 60% to leave headroom for snapshots, compactions, and rebalancing.
attributes.name: [CockroachDB OTel] Storage capacity usage high

investigation_guide.blob:
## CockroachDB storage capacity usage high

### What fired
`capacity_used / capacity` on at least one store exceeded 60% over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
CockroachDB documents 60% as the safe headroom threshold. Above this, the cluster has less room for rebalancing, snapshots for up-replication, and compactions. Above ~90% the node will refuse writes.

### Triage
1. On the Storage dashboard, see which store(s) are highest and whether usage is trending upwards.
2. Is the usage cluster-wide (steady data growth) or concentrated on a subset of stores (rebalancing lag)?
3. Check `ranges` and `replicas` distribution across stores — imbalance will reveal itself here.

### Remediation
- Add nodes/capacity; let the rebalancer spread data.
- Identify and archive or TTL large tables.
- If the imbalance is temporary (decommission, node add), wait for rebalancing to complete; otherwise investigate `rebalancing_range_rebalances` progress.

### Tuning
- The `0.6` (60%) threshold is documented; raise to 0.7 for clusters with aggressive rebalancing SLOs, never higher than 0.8.
- 15-minute evaluation window keeps noise low for a slow-moving metric.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-cockroachdb.otel-default*
| WHERE service.name == "cockroachdb"
  AND capacity IS NOT NULL
  AND capacity > 0
  AND capacity_used IS NOT NULL
// Used should not exceed 60% of total; adjust ratio if needed
| EVAL usage_ratio = capacity_used / capacity
| WHERE usage_ratio > 0.6
| WHERE attributes.node_id IS NOT NULL AND attributes.store IS NOT NULL
| STATS usage_pct = ROUND(MAX(usage_ratio) * 100.0, 1), used_gb = ROUND(MAX(capacity_used) / 1073741824.0, 1), total_gb = ROUND(MAX(capacity) / 1073741824.0, 1)
  BY attributes.node_id, attributes.store, host.name
| SORT usage_pct DESC
